conversations.content: Today we studied the Aurora New Energy Battery Plant High-Risk Operation Hazard Prevention Code. If the electrolyte storage tank pressure exceeds the safety threshold...
Electrolyte tank overpressure? Could explode any moment! How do you relieve the pressure?
then automatically open the pressure relief exhaust valve.
Automatic pressure relief exhaust — good safety design! Any other hazards?
If the humidity in the clean workshop falls below the insulation lower limit, then activate the high-voltage static elimination device.
Humidity too low — risk of static sparks? Static eliminator deployed! Third scenario?
If spark signs appear on the electrode sheet rolling mill rollers, then immediately spray perfluorohexanone fire extinguishing agent.
Rolling mill sparks — spray perfluorohexanone, quick extinguishing! Is there a fourth?
If thermal runaway occurs in a cell during the formation process, then push the faulty module into an explosion-proof water tank for isolation.
Cell thermal runaway — push into explosion-proof water tank, physical isolation and cooling! Understood!

think: Aurora New Energy battery plant hazard prevention: four hazards: electrolyte tank overpressure → pressure relief exhaust valve; clean workshop humidity below insulation limit → high-voltage static elimination device; rolling mill sparks → perfluorohexanone fire extinguishing agent; cell thermal runaway during formation → push faulty module into explosion-proof water tank.
memory: Aurora New Energy Battery Plant High-Risk Operation Hazard Prevention Code: If the electrolyte storage tank pressure exceeds the safety threshold, then automatically open the pressure relief exhaust valve; if the humidity in the clean workshop falls below the insulation lower limit, then activate the high-voltage static elimination device; if spark signs appear on the electrode sheet rolling mill rollers, then immediately spray perfluorohexanone fire extinguishing agent; if thermal runaway occurs in a cell during the formation process, then push the faulty module into an explosion-proof water tank for isolation.
